description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, featuring a domed top and a cylindrical body with a flared base for stability. The hydrant has two prominent side outlets for hose connections and a central front nozzle, all capped and slightly protruding from the main body. The surface is painted a vivid red, with visible patches of weathering and paint wear, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The top of the hydrant includes a small, raised operating nut or cap, used for opening the water valve. The overall construction appears robust, likely made from cast iron or steel, designed to withstand high water pressure and urban wear. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is shown isolated without any curb or sidewalk context.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with a domed top, two side outlets, and a central front nozzle, all capped and robustly built.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central nozzle and domed top, with weathered red paint.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting both hose connection outlets protruding from the cylindrical body.
description_view_2: Opposite front view, again showing the central nozzle and domed top, with less visible weathering.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, displaying both hose connection outlets and the flared base.
